name: Applesauce Cake Baked in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the shortening and sugar until smooth.
2. Beat in each egg, one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
3. Gradually add the applesauce and water; stir to combine.
4. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and cloves.
5. Blend the dry ingredients into the applesauce mixture until just combined.
6. Gently fold in the chopped nuts if using.
7. Sterilize 9-10 Ball Quilted Crystal canning jars by boiling them for 15 minutes. Remove and let air-dry; keep lids and rings in hot water until ready to use.
8. Once jars are cool, lightly grease the inside of each jar with shortening (avoid butter, margarine, or PAM).
9. Fill each jar about halfway full with batter.
10. Place jars on a baking sheet and bake at 325°F for 35-45 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
11. Remove jars from oven one at a time; wipe off any residue around the rim.
12. Replace lids and rings, screwing them on tightly.
13. Allow jars to cool completely before storing in a cool, dark place. They can be stored for up to 12 months.
Note: Jars will seal as they cool. Use heavy-duty oven mitts when handling hot jars.
